# ME3 Core

> Installable ME3 Core personal/business AI assistant scaffold. ME3 Core is an owner-controlled assistant, identity layer, public site, private workspace, and plugin surface for a person or business.

Repository: https://github.com/Soulink-Foundation/me3
Knowledge schema: 2026-05-15.v1
Generated from the typed ME3 knowledge package. This file is public-safe and should not include real secrets, production Cloudflare IDs, hosted subscription billing config, private memory, or customer data.

## Product Knowledge

- What ME3 is: ME3 is an assistant, identity layer, public site, and action surface for a person or business.
- Public and private boundaries: Public me.json/profile data is separate from private memory, provider secrets, tasks, approvals, and local context.
- Consent before action: Sending, publishing, booking, paying, editing local files, and running code require clear owner permission and auditability.
- Core and hosted portability: ME3 Core is owner-controlled and installable. Hosted ME3 should reuse shared capability contracts while keeping billing and managed ops outside Core.

## Core Boundaries

- Public me.json/profile data is separate from private memory, provider secrets, tasks, approvals, and local context.
- Core owns installable assistant runtime, owner profile, me.json output, setup-required integration states, and optional owner-supplied providers.
- Hosted-only ME3 Cloud routes, production infrastructure, managed provider accounts, and hosted subscription billing stay outside the Core public docs artifact.
- Consequential actions such as sending, publishing, booking, paying, editing local files, or running code require visible owner permission and auditability.

## Capability Map

- Core chat (chat.core_reply)
  Category: assistant
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: ME3 can answer questions, reason with the owner, and explain the current Core install when an AI route is configured.
  Approval mode: none
  Side effect: none
  Data boundary: Conversation history is owner-scoped inside the install.
  App/API routes: /api/agent/sandbox, /api/assistant/chat
  Agent tool ids: chat.core_reply
  Requires: ai.chat_provider
  Boundaries: Do not say external work was completed unless a tool or API result confirms it. If AI setup is missing, explain how to configure a provider instead of pretending a live model is active.
- Public identity and me.json (identity.public_profile)
  Category: identity
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: ME3 can publish the owner's public profile and me.json so other systems know who they are and what public actions are authorized.
  Approval mode: owner_approval
  Side effect: internal_write
  Data boundary: Public profile data may be published; private memory must not leak into me.json unless explicitly mapped by the owner.
  App/API routes: /.well-known/me.json, /api/account
  Boundaries: Public me.json is not a dump of private context. Profile edits should be explicit owner changes.
- Public site (sites.public_site)
  Category: sites
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: ME3 can host a simple public site for the owner, including profile, links, booking/contact surfaces, and generated pages.
  Approval mode: owner_approval
  Side effect: internal_write
  Data boundary: Site content is public once published; drafts and setup data remain owner-scoped.
  App/API routes: /api/sites, /sites/:username
  Boundaries: Distinguish drafts from published pages. Do not publish visible changes without owner intent.
- Mission Control workspace (workspace.mission_control)
  Category: workspace
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: Mission Control gives the owner a private workspace for tasks, projects, approvals, private memory, context sources, and run history.
  Approval mode: owner_approval
  Side effect: internal_write
  Data boundary: Mission Control data is private, owner-scoped, and plugin-owned.
  App/API routes: /mission-control, /mission-control/wheel-of-life
  Agent tool ids: mission.task.create, mission.memory.write, mission.approval.request
  Boundaries: Private memory writes should be visible and approval-aware. Public me.json remains Core-owned and separate from Mission Control memory.
- Optional local daemon bridge (workspace.local_daemon_bridge)
  Category: workspace
  Surfaces: core
  Lifecycle: partial
  Runtime state in Core: partial
  Summary: ME3 is designed to pair with an optional local daemon for explicitly approved local files, repos, and shell actions.
  Approval mode: local_access_approval
  Side effect: local_shell
  Data boundary: Local file and repo access must be explicit, scoped, and audited.
  App/API routes: /api/mission-control/daemon/*
  Agent tool ids: mission.daemon.read, mission.daemon.write, mission.daemon.shell
  Requires: mission.daemon_pairing
  Boundaries: Do not claim local access unless the daemon is paired and the requested path/action is allowed. Never make local file or shell access implicit.
- Calendar events and reminders (calendar.events_reminders)
  Category: calendar
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: ME3 can manage native personal calendar events, reminders, bookings, birthdays, imported calendar sources, and recurring event expansion.
  Approval mode: owner_approval
  Side effect: internal_write
  Data boundary: Calendar records are private owner workspace data unless explicitly shared through a public surface.
  App/API routes: /calendar, /api/calendar/feed, /api/agent/reminders
  Agent tool ids: calendar.event.create, calendar.reminder.create
  Boundaries: Be precise about dates, times, and time zones. Do not claim a reminder or event was created without an API/tool result.
- Mailbox drafts and approvals (mailbox.drafts_approvals)
  Category: mailbox
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: ME3 can provide a Core mailbox, draft email replies, track mailbox activity, and require approval before sending outbound mail.
  Approval mode: external_action_approval
  Side effect: external_write
  Data boundary: Mailbox messages and provider tokens are private; provider secrets are encrypted and never returned to the browser.
  App/API routes: /email, /api/mailbox
  Boundaries: Drafting is not sending. Sending email requires owner approval and provider readiness.
- Contacts and relationships (contacts.relationships)
  Category: contacts
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: ME3 can store contacts, relationship status, outreach status, notes, social handles, follow-up dates, and booking links.
  Approval mode: owner_approval
  Side effect: internal_write
  Data boundary: Contacts are private owner workspace data unless explicitly published or exported.
  App/API routes: /api/contacts
  Boundaries: Do not expose private contact notes in public profile output. Be careful with outreach status and follow-up claims.
- Social content publishing (content.social_publishing)
  Category: content
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: setup_required
  Runtime state in Core: setup_required
  Summary: ME3 can manage social content drafts, connected accounts, queue state, and approval-first publishing workflows when the social plugin is installed.
  Approval mode: external_action_approval
  Side effect: external_write
  Data boundary: Drafts and queue state are owner-scoped; external publication reaches connected social platforms.
  App/API routes: /social, /api/content/items, /api/social/accounts
  Agent tool ids: content.write_preview, content.publish
  Requires: social.oauth_accounts, social.publish_queue
  Boundaries: Never represent queued or drafted content as published. External publishing requires owner approval and provider setup.
- Landing page generation (sites.landing_pages)
  Category: sites
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: coming_soon
  Runtime state in Core: coming_soon
  Summary: ME3 has a first-party landing-page package for draft generation and rendering, but the Core catalog currently marks it as coming soon.
  Approval mode: owner_approval
  Side effect: internal_write
  Data boundary: Landing-page drafts are owner-scoped until published.
  App/API routes: /api/agent/landing-pages/generate
  Agent tool ids: landing_pages.generate_draft
  Requires: landing_pages.plugin_enabled
  Boundaries: Call this coming soon unless runtime/plugin state proves it is enabled. Generated drafts are not published pages.
- AI provider routing (ai.chat_provider)
  Category: providers
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: setup_required
  Runtime state in Core: setup_required
  Summary: ME3 can route chat through Workers AI, OpenAI, or Anthropic when the owner configures a provider or binding.
  Approval mode: none
  Side effect: none
  Data boundary: Provider API keys are private install secrets and should not be exposed.
  App/API routes: /account, /api/ai-settings
  Requires: workers_ai_or_api_key
  Boundaries: Do not expose provider secrets. Distinguish configured model routes from fallback shell responses.
- Approvals and audit trail (safety.approvals_audit)
  Category: safety
  Surfaces: core, hosted
  Lifecycle: available
  Runtime state in Core: available
  Summary: ME3 is designed around visible approvals and audit trails for consequential work across plugins and local/hosted surfaces.
  Approval mode: owner_approval
  Side effect: internal_write
  Data boundary: Approvals and audit records are private workspace data.
  App/API routes: /mission-control, /api/mission-control/approvals
  Agent tool ids: mission.approval.request
  Boundaries: Consequential actions should be reviewable. Approval state should be visible rather than hidden in prompt context.

## Source Documents

- [ME3 Core README](https://github.com/Soulink-Foundation/me3/blob/main/README.md): Installable Core overview, product principles, local setup, deploy flow, and Core vs hosted boundaries.
- [ME3 Knowledge README](https://github.com/Soulink-Foundation/me3/blob/main/packages/knowledge/README.md): Explains the shared typed product and capability knowledge package that powers this artifact.
- [ME3 Knowledge Source](https://github.com/Soulink-Foundation/me3/blob/main/packages/knowledge/src/index.ts): Typed product facts, capability map, approval modes, side effects, data boundaries, routes, and agent tool ids.
- [ME3 How-To Guide](https://github.com/Soulink-Foundation/me3/blob/main/docs/how-to-me3.md): Public configuration and troubleshooting guide for ME3 Core installs.
